text.title: User Story Mapping Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding User Story Mapping. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Build the activity backbone (left-to-right journey) before writing any story.
rule.rationale: Build the activity backbone (left-to-right journey) before writing any story.

rule.statement: First horizontal slice is the walking skeleton — minimum end-to-end journey that works.
rule.rationale: First horizontal slice is the walking skeleton — minimum end-to-end journey that works.

rule.statement: Per task, vertical column lists alternative stories ranked top = MVP.
rule.rationale: Per task, vertical column lists alternative stories ranked top = MVP.

rule.statement: Scope cuts are made by drawing horizontal slice lines, not deleting stories.
rule.rationale: Scope cuts are made by drawing horizontal slice lines, not deleting stories.

rule.statement: One persona per map; multiple personas → multiple maps.
rule.rationale: One persona per map; multiple personas → multiple maps.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-user-story-mapping.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
